senses.definition: someone who teaches things to people, particularly in a school

examples.x: I raised my hand to give an answer when the teacher asked a question.
My favorite teacher is very patient and always encourages me to do my best.
The teacher corrected my mistake and explained the correct solution.
The teacher gives the students stickers for their good work.
To enhance our learning experience, our teacher organized a field trip to the museum.

senses.definition: a person who teaches a practical skill or sport to someone

examples.x: I took my driving test after ten lessons with my instructor.
My fitness instructor recommends thirty minutes of exercise every day.
Our scuba diving instructor taught us important safety measures.
She's a yoga instructor at the local community center.
The cooking instructor explained the recipe clearly.

senses.definition: someone whose job is to teach people

examples.x: As an educator, she believes in fostering creativity and critical thinking in her students.
He has dedicated his career to serving as an educator in underserved communities.
The conference brought together educators from around the world to share best practices in teaching.
She was recognized as an outstanding educator for her innovative approach to curriculum development.
The museum offers educational programs led by trained educators to engage visitors of all ages.

senses.definition: a teacher who gives lessons privately to one student or a small group

examples.x: She hired a math tutor to help her daughter improve her grades.
The tutor helped the student prepare for the college entrance exams.
He works as a language tutor, teaching Spanish to beginners.
The tutor specializes in SAT preparation and test-taking strategies.
The tutor tailored the lessons to the student's learning style and pace.

senses.definition: a teacher or instructor, especially one who provides guidance, supervision, and mentorship to students or trainees in a specialized field

examples.x: As a preceptor in the medical residency program, she mentored aspiring doctors in clinical practice.
He served as a preceptor for graduate students, guiding them through their research projects in chemistry.
The preceptor in the law firm provided hands-on training to new associates, preparing them for legal practice.
She sought advice from her preceptor in nursing school to improve her patient care skills.
The preceptor guided the medical students through their clinical rotations.

senses.definition: a person who provides personalized guidance or training in a specific area
an educator who provides training and professional development to other teachers to improve their skills and practices
an academic with a position ranking below professor, often responsible for teaching and research
an experienced teacher at a university or college who specializes in a particular subject and often conducts research

examples.x: He is a professor of physics at a renowned university.
The students waited for the professor to start the lecture.

senses.definition: a professor who has the highest rank in a university

examples.x: Dr. Smith was promoted to full professor after years of dedicated research and teaching.
As a full professor, she mentors graduate students and leads research projects in her field.
The university recognized his contributions to academia by granting him the title of full professor.
Full professors have the responsibility to shape the curriculum and guide departmental policies.
She received tenure and was promoted to full professor in recognition of her scholarly achievements.

senses.definition: someone who gives a lecture or speech, particularly at universities
a university lecturer, typically in European universities, with responsibilities ranging from teaching to research
a member of the university faculty engaged in teaching or research
an official, typically in an academic setting, responsible for supervising exams, maintaining order, and enforcing rules

examples.x: The proctor monitored the students during the final exam.
She reported any instances of cheating to the exam proctor.
The proctor's role is to ensure fairness and integrity during testing.
He sought clarification from the proctor regarding the exam instructions.
The proctor dismissed any student found violating the exam rules.

senses.definition: an official who supervises exams to ensure they are conducted fairly

examples.x: The invigilator circulated around the exam room to monitor the students' behavior.
She checked each student's ID before allowing them to enter the exam hall as an invigilator.
The invigilator reminded the students to keep their desks clear of unauthorized materials.
He reported any suspicious activity to the chief invigilator during the exam.
The university appointed experienced invigilators to oversee the high-stakes entrance exams.

senses.definition: a person responsible for evaluating and assigning grades to students' work, assessments, or exams

examples.x: The grader carefully reviewed each essay submitted by the students before assigning grades.
As a grader, her job involves assessing tests and homework to provide fair evaluations.
The grader's comments on my paper helped me understand where I could improve my writing.
The professor appointed a new grader to assist in managing the workload during exam season.
Graders play an essential role in maintaining academic standards by ensuring consistency in evaluation criteria.

senses.definition: a trained assistant who supports professionals, often in educational or healthcare settings

examples.x: The school employed paraprofessionals to assist teachers in the classroom.
She works as a paraprofessional, providing support to special education students.
The healthcare facility hired paraprofessionals to help with patient care tasks.
He received training to work as a paraprofessional in the counseling department.
Paraprofessionals play a crucial role in facilitating the work of professionals in various fields.

senses.definition: a person who assists pedestrians, especially children, in safely crossing roads or intersections

examples.x: The crossing guard stopped traffic to allow children to cross the street safely.
She volunteered as a crossing guard to ensure the safety of students on their way to school.
The crossing guard held up a stop sign to halt vehicles approaching the intersection.
He donned a bright vest and carried a handheld stop sign as he performed his duties as a crossing guard.
Parents appreciated the presence of the crossing guard, knowing their children were in good hands when crossing the road.

senses.definition: someone who is responsible for advising students about educational and personal decisions
an individual appointed to assist and act on behalf of a higher-ranking official or leader when they are absent

examples.x: The deputy stepped in to lead the team when the manager was absent.
He served as the spokesperson's deputy when he was unavailable.
She acted as the deputy chair of the department during the chair's maternity leave.
The deputy managed state affairs while the governor was away.
He was appointed as the deputy headmaster, assuming leadership duties when the headmaster was on medical leave.

senses.definition: the position that a university professor has
a senior university teacher or professor, particularly associated with Oxford and Cambridge

examples.x: The esteemed don at Oxford University delivered an insightful lecture on medieval history.
She consulted with a renowned don from Cambridge University for guidance on her research project.
The don's published works on quantum mechanics have garnered international acclaim.
He was honored to be appointed as a visiting don at Oxford, where he taught advanced mathematics courses.
The annual conference hosted leading dons from universities across the country to discuss developments in their respective fields.

senses.definition: chief executive officer of a university or college, responsible for overall administration and strategic leadership

examples.x: The chancellor announced the new strategic plan for the university.
She met with alumni donors in her capacity as chancellor to discuss fundraising initiatives.
The chancellor presided over the inauguration of the new campus facility.
He represented the university at national conferences as chancellor, advocating for higher education policies.
The chancellor addressed faculty and staff during the annual state of the university address.

senses.definition: the highest-ranking administrative officer of a university or college
the head of a faculty or a department of studies in a university

examples.x: Dr. Smith has been appointed as the new dean of the Faculty of Arts and Humanities.
The dean is responsible for overseeing the academic programs, faculty, and students within the department.
Under the leadership of the dean, the department has implemented several new initiatives to enhance student learning and research opportunities.
The dean's office serves as a central point of contact for faculty members, students, and external stakeholders.

senses.definition: an administrative officer in a college or university responsible for maintaining student records
the chief academic officer of a college or university
the person in charge of running a school

examples.x: She met with the principal to discuss her child's academic progress and behavior.
The principal announced new school policies during the morning assembly.
The principal's office is located at the end of the hallway on the left.
He admired the principal for her dedication to creating a positive learning environment for all students.
The principal introduced a new program to support teachers in the classroom.

senses.definition: the person in charge of running a school

examples.x: The head of school made important decisions for students and teachers.
Parents met with the head of school to discuss school rules.
The head of school planned new activities for the students.
She became the head of school after years of teaching.
The head of school made sure everything ran smoothly.

senses.definition: the deputy or assistant to a chancellor, often serving as the second-highest executive official in a British university or institution
a school administrator who assists the principal in overseeing the day-to-day operations of the school

examples.x: As the vice-principal in charge of student affairs, she organized extracurricular activities and events to enrich students' experiences.
In the absence of the principal, the vice-principal assumed leadership responsibilities and ensured the smooth operation of the school.

senses.definition: temporarily teaching or conducting research at an institution
